ground_truth.title: Morning and Night Skincare Routine For All Skin Types
ground_truth.author: Skincare.com by L'Oréal
ground_truth.main_content: Starting a skincare routine from scratch can feel overwhelming. You may know the basic skincare steps to follow, but finding the right products isn't always cut-and-dry—especially if you're unsure what skin type you have. Once you find products that work for your skin, you need to figure out what order to apply them in and how much to use. Dr. Dhanusali, board-certified dermatologist, advises that less is more when starting a skincare routine. Using too many products can lead to dryness, irritation, or acne. A good routine doesn't have to be complicated. Stick to the basics: cleanser, serum, moisturizer, and SPF. Apply products by weight, lightest first and heaviest last, with sunscreen always last in the daytime. The morning skincare routine includes four steps: Cleanse with a mild facial cleanser to remove debris and prime skin; Apply serum to deliver key ingredients like hyaluronic acid for hydration; Moisturize with a non-comedogenic lotion; Apply sunscreen with SPF 15 or higher for UV protection. The nighttime routine also starts with cleansing to remove makeup and debris, followed by toning to remove residue, applying serum (anti-aging ingredients like retinoids work well at night), and finishing with a rich night cream. Start with once-weekly retinoid usage and gradually increase frequency as your skin acclimates.
with: Starting a skincare routine from scratch can feel a bit overwhelming.
According to Dr. Dhanusali, board-certified dermatologist, when starting a skincare routine for the first time, less is more.
In terms of skincare order, you typically want to apply your products by weight, with the lightest products going first and the heaviest last.
In the daytime, however, sunscreen should always be last—even if it's lighter than your moisturizer.
Keep in mind that retinoids can take some time to adapt to, so we suggest starting with once-weekly usage and gradually increasing the frequency until your skin acclimates.
without: Beauty Quizzes - Customize your skincare routine
